Basil.growth_info: Basil grows rapidly in hydro — first harvest in 3-4 weeks. Thrives with 16h light. Bolt-prone when temps exceed 85°F. EC can be raised slightly at maturity for more intense flavor.
tips: Pinch off flower buds immediately — bolting ruins leaf flavor
Harvest from the top to encourage bushier, more productive growth
Keep roots oxygenated; basil is highly susceptible to root rot
Nudge EC to 1.4-1.6 just before harvest for slightly more intense aroma

Cilantro.growth_info: Cool-season herb that bolts rapidly above 75°F, degrading leaf flavor. Requires cooler conditions and shorter days than most herbs. Harvest continuously before flowering — flavor drops sharply once the plant begins to bolt.
tips: Limit light to 10-12 hours daily to mimic autumn and delay bolting
Keep water temp below 70°F — warm roots accelerate bolting significantly
Direct-seed into pods rather than transplanting; cilantro hates root disturbance
Harvest outer leaves regularly; once flowering starts, flavor is largely lost

Green Onion.growth_info: Bunching onion with exceptional cut-and-come-again regeneration — shoots regrow within days of cutting. Produces 3-4 harvests per plant cycle. Needs slightly higher pH than most herbs.
tips: Cut 1 inch above the growing medium, never pull — roots must stay intact for regrowth
Start seedlings at EC 0.7-1.2, gradually increase to 2.0-2.5 during bulking
Harvest when stems are 6-8 inches tall with roughly ¼-inch thick bases
Keep water temp 64-68°F for maximum dissolved oxygen and root health

Kale.growth_info: Hardy and forgiving. Baby kale ready in 3-4 weeks. Mature in 8-12 weeks. Higher EC tolerance than most leafy greens.
tips: Harvest outer leaves to keep plant producing
Light frost improves flavor — cooler temps are fine
Higher EC needed for healthy color and thickness
Good companion with most other leafy greens (similar ranges)

Lettuce.growth_info: One of the fastest hydro crops. Baby leaf ready in 2-3 weeks, full head in 4-5 weeks. Bolts above 75°F — keep it cool.
tips: Low EC prevents tip burn on outer leaves
Use 14-16h light; keep temps below 75°F to prevent bolting
Harvest outer leaves to extend plant life
Butterhead varieties are most forgiving for beginners

Mint.growth_info: Aggressive spreader — keep in dedicated pods. Harvest frequently. Prefers stable, slightly cooler temps. Long-lived perennial.
tips: Harvest often to prevent woody stems
Keep isolated — mint spreads roots aggressively
Replace plants every 6 months as older stems become woody
Mild EC increases (to 2.2-2.4 mS/cm) may slightly intensify aroma

Parsley.growth_info: Cold-hardy herb with slow germination but consistent cut-and-come-again production. Italian flat-leaf varieties outperform curly in hydroponic yield. Regrowth cycles occur every 3 weeks after each cut.
tips: Harvest by cutting stems to 2-3 inches above medium; regrowth occurs every 3 weeks
Replace plants after 2-3 harvests (8-12 weeks) as older plants lose vigor
Provide 14-16 hours of light daily for maximum yield
Wide EC and temp tolerance makes parsley very forgiving — great for mixed towers

Pepper (Bell).growth_info: Warm-season crop, slow to mature. Green peppers are unripe bells — leave longer for red/yellow. Needs consistent warmth and pollination.
tips: Needs temps above 70°F consistently — cold slows growth significantly
Increase EC during fruiting for better yield
Shake or use a fan to help pollinate flowers
Remove early flowers on young plants to build root strength first

Pepper (Thai Chili).growth_info: Warm-season hot pepper requiring consistently high temps and a rich nutrient solution during fruiting. Self-pollinating but benefits from airflow around flowers. Compact plant, prolific producer once established.
tips: Shift to low-nitrogen, high-P/K nutrients during flowering and fruiting
Never let temps drop below 65°F at night — cold shock causes flower drop
Shake plants or run a fan near flowers to improve pollination and fruit set
Maintain 40-70% humidity; too dry or too wet during flowering causes bud drop

Spinach.growth_info: Cold-preferring crop. Bolt-prone above 75°F. Higher EC than lettuce; nutrient-dense feeder. Ready in 5-6 weeks.
tips: Needs higher EC than most leafy greens
Keep temps on the cool end for best flavor
Harvest whole plant at 6 weeks or outer leaves continuously
Avoid ammonia-heavy nutrients — spinach prefers nitrate nitrogen

Strawberry.growth_info: Slow to establish (2-4 months to first fruit). Everbearing varieties produce year-round. Sensitive to high EC. Keep runners trimmed.
tips: Use everbearing varieties (Seascape, Albion) for continuous harvest
Remove runners unless you want propagation
Low EC critical — high salinity stunts fruit size
Expose to 12h light minimum for consistent fruiting

Swiss Chard.growth_info: Colorful, cold-tolerant, and prolific. Cut-and-come-again harvesting. Closely related to spinach — similar care requirements.
tips: Harvest outer stalks to keep center growing
Tolerates mild frost — wider temp range than most crops
Rainbow chard varieties are identical care but add color to setup
Good companion with spinach and kale — overlapping optimal ranges

Tomato (Cherry).growth_info: Heavy feeder requiring support (trellising or stakes). EC needs rise as plant matures and fruits. Needs pollination — shake or vibrate flowers daily.
tips: Increase EC to 3.0-3.5 during fruiting for sweeter tomatoes
Prune suckers to focus energy on fruit production
Shake or vibrate flowers daily to self-pollinate
Watch for calcium deficiency (blossom end rot) — check pH carefully

Tomato (Cloudy Day Hybrid).growth_info: Indeterminate hybrid bred to thrive in low-light, cool, and overcast conditions. Excellent blight resistance and consistent yields in indoor or northern settings. Requires trellising and active pruning.
tips: Tolerates lower light than standard tomatoes — 8-10 hours minimum daily
Keep EC at 2.0-2.5 during flowering, raise to 3.0-3.5 during fruiting for sweeter fruit
Shake or vibrate flowers daily for self-pollination; a fan running near blooms helps
Prune suckers regularly — indeterminate growth needs active management to direct energy to fruit

Cucumber.growth_info: Vining plant — needs significant vertical space and trellis. Warm-temp crop. Produces prolifically once established. Needs pollination.
tips: Train vines vertically to save space
Hand-pollinate flowers with a small brush or shake plant
Harvest young (6-8") for best flavor and to encourage more fruit
Needs consistent temps above 65°F — cold shock causes bitter fruit
